_comment: Canonical capture environment for the oracle corpus (DOCS.md §10.10).
Single source of truth shared by capture.sh (host driver) and
capture-runner.sh (in-VM). Everything that would otherwise drift
byte-for-byte across machines is pinned here.
IDENTITY INVARIANT — why this exists:
Oracles are committed with absolute paths baked in (today ~95k
`/Users/hyzyla` strings from the original Mac capture). The resolver's
canonicalizer already strips $HOME / DerivedData-hash / SDK-version, so
structural+canonical tiers pass cross-machine; but a recapture on a
different host still rewrites every path string => unreviewable churn,
and the path-derived DerivedData hash (see commits #287/#288/#289) only
becomes BYTE-stable when the capture path is fixed. Pinning the VM user
(`canonical_home`) and the checkout path (`checkout_path`) makes every
recapture produce identical bytes, so a capture diff shows only real
behaviour deltas.
IMAGE TAGS — verify before a refresh:
The `image` refs are Cirrus Labs Tart images (Xcode preinstalled, user
`admin`, home `/Users/admin`). Tags move; confirm the exact tag exists
at https://github.com/cirruslabs/macos-image-templates (ghcr.io) and
update it here. For an Xcode minor Cirrus does NOT publish, bake your
own from a `macos-<release>-base` image (README.md, 'Baking an image').

26.5.0.notes: Latest non-beta 26.x — full corpus incl. simulator scheme captures.

16.4.0.notes: macOS scheme + per-target + project-defaults; ice-cubes needs Swift-tools 6.2 (26.x).

15.4.0.notes: macOS-only captures; alamofire/netnewswire/ice-cubes walled off by objectVersion / Swift-tools.
